21. The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all.
22.WEB:
1. He showed me a river of water of life, clear as crystal, proceeding out of the throne of God and of the Lamb,
2. in the middle of its street. On this side of the river and on that was the tree of life, bearing twelve kinds of fruits, yielding its fruit every month. The leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations.
3. There will be no curse any more. The throne of God and of the Lamb will be in it, and his servants will serve him.
4. They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads.
5. There will be no night, and they need no lamp light; for the Lord God will illuminate them. They will reign forever and ever.
6. He said to me, “These words are faithful and true. The Lord God of the spirits of the prophets sent his angel to show to his bondservants the things which must happen soon.”
7. “Behold, I come quickly. Blessed is he who keeps the words of the prophecy of this book.”
8. Now I, John, am the one who heard and saw these things. When I heard and saw, I fell down to worship before the feet of the angel who had shown me these things.
9. He said to me, “See you don’t do it! I am a fellow bondservant with you and with your brothers, the prophets, and with those who keep the words of this book. Worship God.”
10. He said to me, “Don’t seal up the words of the prophecy of this book, for the time is at hand.
11. He who acts unjustly, let him act unjustly still. He who is filthy, let him be filthy still. He who is righteous, let him do righteousness still. He who is holy, let him be holy still.”
12. “Behold, I come quickly. My reward is with me, to repay to each man according to his work.
13. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End.
14. Blessed are those who do his commandments, that they may have the right to the tree of life, and may enter in by the gates into the city.
15. Outside are the dogs, the sorcerers, the sexually immoral, the murderers, the idolaters, and everyone who loves and practices falsehood.
16. I, Jesus, have sent my angel to testify these things to you for the assemblies. I am the root and the offspring of David; the Bright and Morning Star.”
17. The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” He who hears, let him say, “Come!” He who is thirsty, let him come. He who desires, let him take the water of life freely.
18. I testify to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book, if anyone adds to them, may God add to him the plagues which are written in this book.
19. If anyone takes away from the words of the book of this prophecy, may God take away his part from the tree of life, and out of the holy city, which are written in this book.
20. He who testifies these things says, “Yes, I come quickly.” Amen! Yes, come, Lord Jesus.
21. The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with all the saints. Amen.
22.notes:
Revelation - Chapter 22
Bible Study Notes
========================================


CHAPTER SUMMARY
------------------------------------
The river of life flows from God's throne, the tree of life bears twelve fruits, His servants see His face, and Jesus declares "Behold, I come quickly" — the Spirit and the Bride say "Come," and Scripture's final prayer: "Even so, come, Lord Jesus."


AUTHORSHIP & HISTORICAL BACKGROUND
------------------------------------
Author: The Apostle John (see Chapter 1 notes for full details).

Historical Context: Chapter 22 is the final chapter of the Bible. It
completes the vision of the New Jerusalem (vv.1-5), then provides the
epilogue to the entire book of Revelation (vv.6-21). The chapter brings the
Bible full circle: Genesis opened with a garden, a tree of life, a river, and
God's presence — all lost through sin. Revelation closes with a city-garden,
the tree of life restored, a river of life, and God's presence regained
forever. The curse is reversed. Paradise is restored.

The River and Tree of Life (vv.1-5): A pure river of water of life flows from
God's throne (v.1). The tree of life on both sides of the river — bearing
twelve fruits monthly, leaves for healing nations (v.2). No more curse (v.3).
God's servants serve Him, see His face, bear His name (vv.3-4). No night —
God is their light — they reign forever (v.5).

The Epilogue (vv.6-21): "These sayings are faithful and true" (v.6). "Behold,
I come quickly" — three times (vv.7, 12, 20). Blessed are those who keep this
prophecy (v.7). John warned not to worship the angel (vv.8-9). "Seal not the
sayings" — the time is near (v.10). Christ comes with reward (v.12). "I am
Alpha and Omega" (v.13). The final invitation: "The Spirit and the bride say,
Come... whosoever will, let him take the water of life freely" (v.17). Warning
against adding to or taking from this book (vv.18-19). "Surely I come quickly.
Amen. Even so, come, Lord Jesus" (v.20). Grace (v.21).


COMMENTARY REFERENCES
------------------------------------
Enduring Word (David Guzik):
 https://enduringword.com/bible-commentary/revelation-22/
 Guzik covers the river and tree of life, the no-more-curse promise, the
 final invitation, and the Bible's closing words.

Charles Spurgeon:
 Sermon: "Come, Lord Jesus" on v.20 (MTP Vol. 22, No. 1326)
 "Even so, come, Lord Jesus — the last prayer of the Bible. The church's
 deepest longing expressed in four words. Come — we are ready. Come — we are
 waiting. Come — we are longing. Come, Lord Jesus. Not 'come eventually' but
 'come quickly.' This should be the prayer of every believer: not fear of
 His coming but desire for it. Even so — yes, truly, absolutely — come."
 Sermon: "Whosoever Will" on v.17 (MTP Vol. 30, No. 1808)
 "Whosoever WILL — the widest possible invitation. No one excluded by God
 who is not excluded by their own unwillingness. The Spirit says come. The
 bride says come. The hearer says come. The thirsty are invited. Whosoever
 wills may take. Freely — without cost, without merit, without condition
 except willingness. The Bible ends not with a threat but with an invitation.
 Come."


TRANSLATION NOTES
------------------------------------
Verse On Reading | Translation | Wording
-----------------|-------------|--------
v.1               | KJV         | "a pure river of water of life, clear as crystal, proceeding out of the throne of God and of the Lamb" — Both equivalent. ESV uses "bright" for Greek "lampron" (shining/ radiant) rather than KJV's "clear." Greek "hudatos zōēs" — living water. The river flows from "the throne of God AND of the Lamb" — Father and Son together as the source of life.
v.1               | ESV         | "the river of the water of life, bright as crystal, flowing from the throne of God and of the Lamb" — Both equivalent. ESV uses "bright" for Greek "lampron" (shining/ radiant) rather than KJV's "clear." Greek "hudatos zōēs" — living water. The river flows from "the throne of God AND of the Lamb" — Father and Son together as the source of life.
v.2               | KJV         | "the tree of life, which bare twelve manner of fruits, and yielded her fruit every month: and the leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations" — Both equivalent. Greek "xulon zōēs" — the tree of life from Genesis 2:9, now restored. "Healing" — Greek "therapeian" — health/wholeness, not curing sickness but providing ongoing vitality.
v.2               | ESV         | "the tree of life with its twelve kinds of fruit, yielding its fruit each month. The leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations" — Both equivalent. Greek "xulon zōēs" — the tree of life from Genesis 2:9, now restored. "Healing" — Greek "therapeian" — health/wholeness, not curing sickness but providing ongoing vitality.
v.3               | KJV         | "there shall be no more curse" — ESV's "anything accursed" (Greek "katathema") is more expansive than KJV's "no more curse." The curse of Genesis 3:17 is permanently reversed. Everything that sin corrupted is restored.
v.3               | ESV         | "No longer will there be anything accursed" — ESV's "anything accursed" (Greek "katathema") is more expansive than KJV's "no more curse." The curse of Genesis 3:17 is permanently reversed. Everything that sin corrupted is restored.
v.4               | KJV         | "they shall see his face; and his name shall be in their foreheads" — Both equivalent. The beatific vision. In the OT, no one could see God's face and live (Exodus 33:20). In eternity, believers see Him face to face — the ultimate intimacy.
v.4               | ESV         | "They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads" — Both equivalent. The beatific vision. In the OT, no one could see God's face and live (Exodus 33:20). In eternity, believers see Him face to face — the ultimate intimacy.
v.5               | KJV         | "there shall be no night there; and they need no candle, neither light of the sun; for the Lord God giveth them light: and they shall reign for ever and ever" — ESV uses "lamp" for KJV's "candle" (Greek "luchnos"). Both equivalent in meaning.
v.5               | ESV         | "And night will be no more. They will need no light of lamp or sun, for the Lord God will be their light, and they will reign forever and ever" — ESV uses "lamp" for KJV's "candle" (Greek "luchnos"). Both equivalent in meaning.
v.7               | KJV         | "Behold, I come quickly: blessed is he that keepeth the sayings of the prophecy of this book" — ESV uses "soon" for Greek "tachu" rather than KJV's "quickly." The word can mean either "soon" or "suddenly."
v.7               | ESV         | "And behold, I am coming soon. Blessed is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy of this book" — ESV uses "soon" for Greek "tachu" rather than KJV's "quickly." The word can mean either "soon" or "suddenly."
v.11              | KJV         | "He that is unjust, let him be unjust still: and he which is filthy, let him be filthy still: and he that is righteous, let him be righteous still: and he that is holy, let him be holy still" — ESV uses "evildoer" for KJV's "unjust." Both convey the finality of character at judgment — no more opportunity to change.
v.11              | ESV         | "Let the evildoer still do evil, and the filthy still be filthy, and the righteous still do right, and the holy still be holy" — ESV uses "evildoer" for KJV's "unjust." Both convey the finality of character at judgment — no more opportunity to change.
v.12              | KJV         | "my reward is with me, to give every man according as his work shall be" — ESV uses "recompense" (Greek "misthos" — wages/ reward) and "repay" rather than KJV's "reward" and "give."
v.12              | ESV         | "bringing my recompense with me, to repay each one for what he has done" — ESV uses "recompense" (Greek "misthos" — wages/ reward) and "repay" rather than KJV's "reward" and "give."
v.13              | KJV         | "I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, the first and the last" — Note the different order: KJV puts "beginning and end" second; ESV puts "first and last" second. Three pairs expressing the same truth: Christ encompasses all of reality.
v.13              | ESV         | "I am the Alpha and the Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end" — Note the different order: KJV puts "beginning and end" second; ESV puts "first and last" second. Three pairs expressing the same truth: Christ encompasses all of reality.
v.14              | KJV         | "Blessed are they that do his commandments, that they may have right to the tree of life" — Significant textual difference. KJV follows the Textus Receptus ("do his commandments" — Greek "poiountes tas entolas autou"). ESV follows earlier manuscripts ("wash their robes" — Greek "plunontes tas stolas autōn"). The difference is one letter in Greek.
v.14              | ESV         | "Blessed are those who wash their robes, so that they may have the right to the tree of life" — Significant textual difference. KJV follows the Textus Receptus ("do his commandments" — Greek "poiountes tas entolas autou"). ESV follows earlier manuscripts ("wash their robes" — Greek "plunontes tas stolas autōn"). The difference is one letter in Greek.
v.16              | KJV         | "I am the root and the offspring of David, and the bright and morning star" — ESV uses "descendant" for Greek "genos" (offspring/descendant). Christ is both David's ancestor (root — He created David) and David's descendant (offspring — born from David's line). "The bright morning star" — the star that announces the dawn.
v.16              | ESV         | "I am the root and the descendant of David, the bright morning star" — ESV uses "descendant" for Greek "genos" (offspring/descendant). Christ is both David's ancestor (root — He created David) and David's descendant (offspring — born from David's line). "The bright morning star" — the star that announces the dawn.
v.17              | KJV         | "the Spirit and the bride say, Come... And whosoever will, let him take the water of life freely" — ESV uses "without price" (Greek "dōrean" — as a gift, without payment) for KJV's "freely." Greek "ho thelōn" — the one who wills/ desires. The final invitation is to anyone who wants to come.
v.17              | ESV         | "The Spirit and the Bride say, 'Come.'... let the one who desires take the water of life without price" — ESV uses "without price" (Greek "dōrean" — as a gift, without payment) for KJV's "freely." Greek "ho thelōn" — the one who wills/ desires. The final invitation is to anyone who wants to come.
v.19              | KJV         | "God shall take away his part out of the book of life" — Significant textual difference. KJV reads "book of life" (Textus Receptus); ESV reads "tree of life" (Greek "xulou" — tree, following earlier manuscripts).
v.19              | ESV         | "God will take away his share in the tree of life" — Significant textual difference. KJV reads "book of life" (Textus Receptus); ESV reads "tree of life" (Greek "xulou" — tree, following earlier manuscripts).
v.20              | KJV         | "Surely I come quickly. Amen. Even so, come, Lord Jesus" — ESV uses "soon" for "quickly." Greek "Nai, erchou, Kurie Iēsou" — Yes, come, Lord Jesus. The church's final prayer. Echoes the Aramaic "Maranatha" (1 Cor 16:22).
v.20              | ESV         | "Surely I am coming soon." Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!" — ESV uses "soon" for "quickly." Greek "Nai, erchou, Kurie Iēsou" — Yes, come, Lord Jesus. The church's final prayer. Echoes the Aramaic "Maranatha" (1 Cor 16:22).
v.21              | KJV         | "The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen." — ESV omits "Christ" and "you" (following shorter manuscript readings). The Bible's final word is GRACE. From Genesis to Revelation, grace has the last word.
v.21              | ESV         | "The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all. Amen." — ESV omits "Christ" and "you" (following shorter manuscript readings). The Bible's final word is GRACE. From Genesis to Revelation, grace has the last word.
v.1               | NET         | "Then the angel showed me the river of the water of life — Loss clear as crystal — flowing from the throne of God and of the Lamb" — "water of life" (hydatos zOEs). WEB: "a river of water of life, clear as crystal." Echoing Ezekiel 47 and Genesis 2 — paradise restored and surpassed.
v.2               | NET         | "On each side of the river is the tree of life producing twelve kinds of fruit, yielding its fruit every month" — "tree of life" (xylon zOEs = wood/tree of life). WEB: "the tree of life." Genesis 3 barred access; Revelation 22 restores it — no more cherubim guarding the way.
v.4               | NET         | "They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads" — "see his face" (opsontai to prosOpon autou). WEB: "They will see his face." The beatific vision — what Moses was denied (Exodus 33:20) believers receive eternally. Face-to-face with God.
v.17              | NET         | "And the Spirit and the bride say, 'Come!' And let the one who hears say, 'Come!' And let the one who is thirsty come; let the one who wants it take the water of life free of charge" — "free of charge" (dOrean = as a gift). WEB: "let him who desires take the water of life freely." The final gospel invitation — free, universal, Spirit-empowered.
v.20              | NET         | "The one who testifies to these things says, 'Yes, I am coming soon!' Amen! Come, Lord Jesus!" — "Come, Lord Jesus" (erchou kyrie IEsou — Maranatha in Aramaic). WEB: "Amen! Yes, come, Lord Jesus." KJV: "Even so, come, Lord Jesus." The last prayer of the Bible — the church's longing across two millennia, still unanswered, still urgent.

GLOSSARY
------------------------------------
Term              | Definition                                                                                                                  | Notes
------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------
River of water of life| Eternal life flowing from God's throne. Unending                                                                   | 
Tree of life      | Restored from Eden. Bearing fruit perpetually, providing                                                           | 
No more curse     | The complete reversal of Genesis 3. Sin's consequences                                                             | 
See His face      | The beatific vision. Face-to-face intimacy with God —                                                              | 
Alpha and Omega   | Christ as the beginning and end of all things. He                                                                  | 
Bright and morning star| Christ as the herald of eternal day. The star                                                                      | 
Whosoever will    | The Bible's final invitation. Open to anyone who desires.                                                          | 
Come, Lord Jesus  | The church's final prayer. Longing for Christ's return.                                                            | 
Grace             | The Bible's last word. From creation to consummation, grace                                                        | 

PERSONAL REFLECTION & APPLICATION
------------------------------------
1. "There shall be no more curse" (v.3). Everything sin broke will be
   fixed. Every consequence of the fall — reversed. What aspect of the
   curse do you most long to see removed?

2. "They shall see his face" (v.4). Face-to-face with God — the ultimate
   destiny. Does this hope draw you? Is seeing God's face your deepest
   desire?

3. "Behold, I come quickly" (v.12). Christ's return is imminent. Are you
   ready? Are you living in anticipation? Can you say with John, "Even so,
   come, Lord Jesus"?

4. "Whosoever will, let him take the water of life freely" (v.17). The
   Bible's final invitation is open to anyone who will come. Have you come?
   Are you inviting others to come?

5. "The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all" (v.21). The Bible
   ends with grace. Whatever you face, grace is the final word. God's grace
   is with you — today, tomorrow, and forever.

6. The Bible begins with paradise lost (Genesis 1-3) and ends with paradise
   restored (Revelation 21-22). The whole story is about God bringing His
   people home. You are part of that story. How does knowing the ending
   change how you live the middle?
